summary: Full backend (pytest, 11/11) and frontend (Playwright E2E) testing of GitHub Profile Analyzer. All review-requested flows pass: health, analyze, cache hit, force refresh, history, get/delete stored profile, 404 for nonexistent user, MySQL persistence verified via history/profile endpoints. Frontend: search, profile rendering (overview/stats/top repos/languages chart), history sidebar updates and click-to-reload, cache badge transitions (fresh -> cached), refresh button forces re-fetch, invalid username displays error toast 'GitHub user not found'.
critical_code_review_comments: github_service.fetch_and_analyze only fetches the first 100 most-recently-updated repos (per_page=100, no pagination). Users with >100 public repos will have incomplete totals - acceptable for cached aggregate insights but worth documenting.

Aggregate stats exclude forks (own_repos filter) but public_repos in the response is the raw GitHub count including forks - mildly inconsistent (e.g. total_repos_analyzed=8 vs public_repos=8 for octocat is fine, but could diverge for users with many forks).
DELETE /api/profile/{username} returns 404 for missing rows; on the frontend deletion path no destructive confirmation is required - acceptable since the row is just a cache.
MYSQL_URL is read via os.environ['MYSQL_URL'] (KeyError on miss) which is good; CACHE_TTL_SECONDS uses int() on env value without try/except (would raise on bad config, but acceptable fail-fast).
test_credentials: Not applicable - no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: Tests use octocat (public GitHub user). All test rows are cleaned via DELETE before/after. No persistent seed data introduced beyond the cache rows that any normal usage would create.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_github_analyzer.py covers all 11 endpoints/flows including MySQL persistence via Create->GET pattern. Frontend Playwright covers full happy path + invalid user. GitHub unauthenticated rate limit is 60/hr; if tests start to flake with 429s, use force=true sparingly and rely on cache. REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in frontend/.env is https://gh-insights-api.preview.emergentagent.com (differs from the URL in the original review request, but is the source of truth from env).
